_about: R&S RTO6 SCPI commands used by scope_module, verified against the RTO6 User Manual (1801.6687.02-13). 'page' = page number in that manual. This is a human reference; the driver in driver.py is the source of truth for what is actually sent.

transport._note: PyVISA over HiSLIP is R&S's recommended high-throughput LAN protocol; raw socket also works.

*IDN?.desc: Identification query: manufacturer, model, serial, firmware.

*CLS.desc: Clear status: empties the event registers and error queue.

*OPC.desc: Sets the OPC bit (ESR bit 0) when all pending operations finish. Used as 'SINGle;*OPC' then poll *ESR? for an abortable wait.

*OPC?.desc: Blocks and returns 1 when operations complete. Manual's examples use 'SINGle;*OPC?'. We avoid the blocking form so acquisitions stay abortable.

*ESR?.desc: Reads and clears the standard event status register. Bit 0 (0x01) is Operation Complete.

FORMat[:DATA].used: REAL,32 (samples already in volts; simplest). INT,16 returns raw counts and needs scaling.

EXPort:WAVeform:INCXvalues.used: Forced OFF so CHANnel:DATA? returns Y-values only; the time axis is rebuilt from the header. If ON, X and Y come back interleaved.

CHANnel<ch>[:WAVeform<wf>]:TYPE.notes: PDETect (peak detect) records the min and max of each sample interval → 2 values per sample, so a reduced record length still captures true pulse peaks. HRESolution = boxcar average; RMS = root-mean-square per interval.

CHANnel<ch>[:WAVeform<wf>]:ARIThmetics.notes: Across-acquisition combination (with ACQuire:COUNt). OFF = raw single-shot; AVERage = mean of N acquisitions; ENVelope = accumulated min/max. Forced OFF by configure() so captures are deterministically raw regardless of front-panel state. Distinct from decimation (TYPE), which reduces within one acquisition.

TIMebase:RANGe.notes: Total acquisition time window. RANGe = 10 * SCALe.

ACQuire:POINts:AUTO.notes: Selects which quantity is held constant when the time scale changes. RECLength pins record length (resolution adapts) — used to fix a point budget over 1 s. NOT an ON/OFF toggle.

ACQuire:POINts[:VALue].notes: With AUTO RECLength + a fixed RANGe, resolution = RANGe / POINts (e.g. 1 s / 1e6 = 1 µs → 1 MSa/s effective).

ACQuire:RESolution.notes: Time between waveform points; alternative to POINts.

ACQuire:POINts:ARATe?.notes: ADC sample rate (points sampled per second).

ACQuire:COUNt.notes: Number of acquisitions for a SINGle/averaging run. Default 1 = one acquisition.

SINGle.notes: Run ACQuire:COUNt acquisitions then stop. We send 'SINGle;*OPC' and poll *ESR? bit 0.

STOP.notes: Stop acquisition. Used to abort a polled single acquisition.

CHANnel<ch>[:WAVeform<wf>]:DATA:HEADer?.notes: ValuesPerSample is 2 for peak-detect/envelope (interleaved min,max), else 1. [:WAVeform<wf>] omitted = waveform 1.

CHANnel<ch>[:WAVeform<wf>]:DATA[:VALues].notes: Y-values per FORMat[:DATA] (when INCXvalues OFF). Offset/Length allow chunked reads. Returned as an IEEE-488.2 binary block under REAL,32/INT,16.

typical_sequence: CHANnel2:DATA?          # binary block of REAL,32 Y-values
